title: Where Are the City Trees? Monitoring Urban Trees across the U.S. Using Generative AI
authors: Firoze, A., Uppala, A., Darling, L., Yeh, R. A., Benes, B., Hardiman, B., Fei, S., & Aliaga, D.
abstract: Urban trees provide critical ecosystem services, yet comprehensive monitoring across the United States has remained elusive. This paper presents a novel approach using generative AI to monitor and map urban trees at an unprecedented scale, leveraging satellite imagery and deep learning techniques.

title: Building Instance Segmentation for Dense Urban Settlements
authors: Firoze, A., Yeh, R. A., & Aliaga, D.
abstract: A method for building instance segmentation in dense urban settlements, addressing challenges of closely packed structures in developing regions.

title: Self-Supervised Large Scale Point Cloud Completion for Archaeological Site Restoration
authors: Li, A., Zimmer-Dauphinee, J., Kalyanam, R., Lindsay, I., VanValkenburgh, P., Wernke, S. A., & Aliaga, D.
venue: IEEE/CVF Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition (CVPR)
abstract: We present a self-supervised approach for large-scale point cloud completion applied to archaeological site restoration, enabling the digital reconstruction of ancient structures from incomplete 3D scans.
